title: Causal sequence teacher as an ensemble feature
summary: Public F1 pit-stop decision research uses time-series deep models because pit decisions depend on recent trajectory, not only a row snapshot.
rationale: Public F1 pit-stop decision research uses time-series deep models because pit decisions depend on recent trajectory, not only a row snapshot. A causal sequence teacher can add diversity to the current tabular AutoGluon-style approach.
implementation_hint: Build rolling windows of the last 5-8 laps per driver-race-stint with numeric deltas, compound, position, and pit history. Train a small GRU/TCN or lag-stacked LightGBM using race/year folds, then feed out-of-fold teacher probabilities into the main tabular model or blend predictions.
expected_effect: May capture abrupt pre-pit signatures such as worsening residual pace or position loss that hand-built window features miss.
risk: Bidirectional models or windows built before splitting can leak future information; class imbalance can make the teacher overconfident without grouped CV.
